Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue grew 14 percent year-over-year supported by enterprise demand for our cloud platform and continued adoption of productivity offerings across commercial and consumer segments.

Operating expenses rose substantially as we accelerated artificial intelligence infrastructure investment and expanded research and development headcount to meet growing demand ahead of planned product launches.

Cash flow from operations remained strong during the quarter, providing flexibility for capital returns through share repurchases and dividends, as well as continued strategic investment.

A new geopolitical tension in key international markets has created uncertainty that may impact sales cycles and customer purchasing decisions in certain regions during the upcoming quarter.

Our Intelligent Cloud segment delivered double-digit revenue growth driven by Azure and server products, reflecting continued customer migration from on-premises workloads to cloud-native architectures.

Item 3. Other Information
